data.narrative:
Qilimanjaro is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 quantum computing peers—3 public (IonQ, Rigetti, D-Wave) and 8 private entities including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total raised ($10M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $20M. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the quantum computing set; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) produced the required minimum of 3 datapoints. Deploying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M (low) to $23M (high), with a median point of $20M. This reflects the constraints of a single applicable methodology and limited peer density at Qilimanjaro's stage and sector. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct comparables at equivalent funding stages, making stage-progression assumptions the primary lever. Future valuations will benefit materially from improved comp visibility as additional Series A and Series B quantum companies reach disclosure or public markets.
